ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF JOEL DROTTS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 302) HEARING SET FOR FEB-21-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 206

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOEL DROTTS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL. TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 21, 2019 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. MR. DROTTS'S MOTION SEEKS TO SET ASIDE THE JANUARY 9, 2019 ORDER DISMISSING THE ENTIRE ACTION PURSUANT TO GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 68608(B) DUE TO HIS FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS TO SHOW CAUSE. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Le T. Duong of the law offices of Reed Smith LLP (415-659-4804) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by the moving party as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all causes of action in the second amended complaint alleged against it is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the causes of action for negligent misrepresentation and concealment and overruled as to all other causes of action. As to the claim to amend the trust, while some of the requested relief is questionable (e.g. hire plaintiff as an auditor), plaintiff Joel Drotts alleges sufficient facts to show that the administrative terms of the trust may need to be modified to effectuate the trust. (Tex. Prop. Code 112.054(a).) Taking Mr. Drotts' allegations as true, the advancement term may need to be fine-tuned to prevent self-dealing. As to the cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, Mr. Drotts has leave to allege injury independent from the breach of the trust document and to allege reliance. The SAC does not clearly allege reliance. The gist of the claim is that defendant made false statements regarding the trust's worth, but Mr. Drotts never believed those statements. As to the fraudulent transfer cause of action, Mr. Drotts alleges that defendant transferred oil leases for as little as $10 (pars. 65-72) and defendant had an intent to hinder. (Par. 70). Mr. Drotts is given leave to re-plead the concealment claim as a breach of fiduciary duty claim. The claim refers to Ex. 4, which is not attached to the second amended complaint. The cited exhibit is an e-mail that merely shows that defendant stated that it could not value Mr. Drotts' shares. Mr. Drotts does not clearly allege that defendant had the ability to share such information or cite other specific information that the defendant trustee should have disclosed. The demurrer to the last six causes of action 5-10 are overruled. The documents attached to Mr. Drotts' pleadings (e.g. accounting report) do not indisputably show that defendant trustee was acting properly. The court takes Mr. Drotts' allegations as true that the trustee engaged in wrongdoing. Contested factual issues cannot be resolved on demurrer. Plaintiff shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. If after a reasonable amount of time the plaintiff does not submit a proposed order, defendant may do so.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joel Drotts, in pro per, Tel. 415.936.1354, for plaintiffs;
Le T. Duong, Reed Smith LLP, Tel. 415.659.4804, for defendants Barbara Denton & Bank of New York Mellon;
Kevin M. Benedicto, of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP, Tel. 415.442.1340, specially appearing for Anadarko;
Greg A. Christianson, of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP, Tel. 415.442.1249, specially appearing for Anadarko.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, BARBARA DENTON DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all sixteen causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint, filed by plaintiff Joel Drotts, is sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to all sixteen causes of action. It appears from Mr. Drotts' opposition memorandum that he desires to file a second amended complaint and he is granted leave to do so. In preparing his second amended complaint, Mr. Drotts is admonished to keep in mind the mandate of CCP 425.10(a)(1) that a complaint must contain "A statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." The first amended complaint runs afoul of this requirement in numerous respects, not least of which is that the allegations are repetitive, occasionally unintelligible, and it is often not clear what the purported legal bases are for the claims alleged. The second amended complaint should also avoid argument and lengthy quotations of statutes and legal rules. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
